reasons: Tigreal can group and knock up enemies, giving Vexana a clean window to land Eternal Guard and follow-up magic damage.
Vexana can add airborne follow-up and summon pressure after Tigreal starts the teamfight.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates the grouped crowd-control setup, while Vexana adds airborne follow-up and summon pressure.
worksBestWhen: Vexana waits for Tigreal's engage before casting ultimate.
The fight happens in a narrow area.
failureCases: Tigreal engages out of Vexana's range.
Enemy assassins dive Vexana before the combo.

reasons: Atlas can pull enemies together, making Vexana's area control and Eternal Guard placement easier to convert.
Vexana can punish the grouped pull area with magic control and summon pressure.

proof.summary: Atlas compresses the enemy formation, while Vexana punishes the grouped area with airborne and summon pressure.
worksBestWhen: Vexana keeps enough distance to avoid counter-engage.
failureCases: Atlas pulls enemies away from Vexana.
Enemy disengage separates the grouped targets.

reasons: Johnson's crash can force a fight start, giving Vexana a direct target area for Eternal Guard and follow-up control.
Vexana can follow the crash with area control while enemies are disrupted.

proof.summary: Johnson creates the sudden engage, while Vexana follows with area control and summon pressure.
worksBestWhen: Johnson crashes into grouped or priority targets.
Vexana is close enough to cast immediately.
failureCases: Johnson crashes too far from Vexana.
Vexana lacks vision and arrives late.
The enemy collapses onto Vexana after Johnson commits.

reasons: Franco can isolate one enemy, allowing Vexana to layer control and summon damage on a stationary target.
Vexana can save Eternal Guard for Franco's confirmed catch.

proof.summary: Franco catches or suppresses a target, while Vexana adds magic burst and Eternal Guard follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Franco pulls a priority target into Vexana range.
Vexana saves ultimate for confirmed catch.
failureCases: Vexana casts late after the target escapes.

reasons: Belerick can taunt and hold enemies near him, giving Vexana time to land delayed area skills and summon pressure.
Vexana can cast from safer distance while Belerick disrupts enemies in front.

proof.summary: Belerick disrupts enemies in front, while Vexana follows with magic control from safer distance.
worksBestWhen: Belerick engages before Vexana commits cooldowns.
failureCases: Belerick is ignored and enemies dive Vexana.
The fight moves away from Eternal Guard.
